title: Flash Loan Oracle Manipulation via Arbitrary CPI
summary: The exec_callback instruction allows arbitrary cross-program invocation without program ID validation. Combined with price oracle reads, an attacker can execute a flash loan to manipulate token prices, borrow against inflated collateral, and repay the flash loan - all in a single transaction.

flash_loan_capital.note: Flash loans on Solana require existing liquidity in lending markets. Attacker borrows from lending protocol, manipulates price, borrows more, repays initial loan.

transaction_fees.note: High CU usage due to complex CPI chain

profit_margin.note: Need sufficient price movement to cover fees + profit

complexity.explanation: Requires understanding of flash loan mechanics, oracle price feeds, and complex multi-hop CPI. Mechanical execution is automated once attack vector is identified.

notes: Flash loans are common in Solana DeFi (Solend, Port, Mango, etc.)
Oracle manipulation requires understanding price feed mechanics
Hard to detect - looks like legitimate DeFi activity
MEV on Solana is less mature than Ethereum - less competition
Audit tools catch arbitrary CPI patterns (Rule 4)

solana_protocols.name: Mango Markets
solana_protocols.note: Most flexible - built into Mango's architecture

preconditions: Target program reads price from manipulable oracle (Pyth, Switchboard, or AMM-based)
Target program uses balance snapshots that can be manipulated
Attacker has sufficient collateral on lending protocol for flash loan collateral

step_by_step.name: Initiate flash loan
step_by_step.action: Call lending protocol's flash loan instruction
step_by_step.note: Flash loan is atomic - must be repaid within same transaction

step_by_step.name: Use arbitrary CPI to manipulate oracle
step_by_step.action: Call exec_callback with target_program = DEX or Token Program
step_by_step.result: Price of collateral asset on DEX moves 10-30%

step_by_step.name: Wait for oracle update
step_by_step.action: Pyth or Switchboard updates price feed
step_by_step.note: May require multiple txs or use AMM spot price directly
step_by_step.alternative: Use AMM spot price as direct oracle (no delay)

step_by_step.name: Borrow against inflated collateral
step_by_step.action: Call target program's borrow with manipulated price
step_by_step.result: Attacker borrows more than legitimate collateral value

step_by_step.name: Repay flash loan
step_by_step.result: Flash loan closed, attacker's net position = stolen funds

mechanics.root_cause: exec_callback accepts any program ID and invokes it without validation (Rule 4)
oracle_manipulation: AMM-based: Swap large amount to move spot price
Pyth: Attacker is price publisher (unlikely but possible)
TWAP: Requires multiple slots (not single-tx flash loan)
mechanics.balance_snapshot_timing: Solana programs read balances at instruction start. Flash loan increases balance, borrow sees inflated collateral, repay decreases balance - all atomic.

commit_reveal.description: Price commitment from prior slot prevents same-tx manipulation
commit_reveal.implementation: Store price_hash from slot N-2, verify in slot N

twap_oracle.limitation: Requires block-space for updates, adds latency

external_validation.description: Price from Pyth/Switchboard with confidence threshold
external_validation.limitation: Still manipulable with large trades if no TWAP

balance_snapshots.description: Take balance snapshot AFTER all state changes and repayments
balance_snapshots.implementation: Check collateral_value at instruction end, not start

on_chain_signals: Large flash loan in same tx as large borrow
Collateral price deviation > 20% within single transaction
Multiple CPI calls with different programs in single tx
off_chain_signals: Unusual borrowing patterns correlating with price moves
tools: Triton Trader bot for MEV monitoring
SolanaFM tx explorer for multi-hop CPI detection

remediation.immediate: Add program allowlist to exec_callback (Rule 4 fix)
oracle_protection: Use TWAP instead of spot price
Add slot-age verification for price feeds
remediation.balance_timing: Take balance snapshots AFTER all repayments in instruction flow
remediation.defense_in_depth: Combine all three - program allowlist + protected oracle + proper snapshot timing

poc_verification.expected_behavior: Should FAIL if target program not in allowlist after fix
poc_verification.attack_scenario: Attempt arbitrary CPI to system program - should fail after allowlist is added
